evals.query: Create a declarative Kora HTTP client UserApiClient for a User API: GET /users/{id}, GET /users with page and size query params, POST /users, and DELETE /users/{id}. The base URL must come from configuration.
expected_behavior: Declares an interface annotated with @HttpClient and uses configPath, with the url supplied under httpClient.* in config
Annotates each method with @HttpRoute(method = HttpMethod.GET/POST/DELETE, path = ...)
Maps path variables with @Path and query parameters with @Query
Uses @Json on the method and on the body parameter for JSON request/response, and adds json-module
Does not put a baseUrl attribute on @HttpClient (that attribute does not exist; url is a config key)

evals.query: Set up the OkHttp transport for a Kora HTTP client: connectTimeout 5s, readTimeout 30s, HTTP/2, with logging, metrics and tracing telemetry, plus a per-client block for usersApi pointing at http://users-service:8080 with a 10s requestTimeout.
expected_behavior: Adds implementation ru.tinkoff.kora:http-client-ok and the annotation processor (annotation-processors for Java or symbol-processors for Kotlin)
Plugs OkHttpClientModule into the @KoraApp interface
Configures httpClient.ok.httpVersion = HTTP_2, connectTimeout and readTimeout, and telemetry.logging/metrics/tracing
Adds a per-client block httpClient.usersApi with url and requestTimeout
Does not version individual ru.tinkoff.kora artifacts and relies on the kora-parent BOM

evals.query: I need to read the HTTP status code and a response header on a Kora HTTP client call instead of just the body. How do I do that?
expected_behavior: Returns HttpResponseEntity<T> from the @HttpRoute method
Reads the status with response.code() and the body with response.body()
Reads headers with response.headers()
Explains that without HttpResponseEntity a non-2xx status throws HttpClientResponseException
Does not use a status() method or invent a ResponseEntity from another framework

evals.query: Implement error handling for a Kora HTTP client UserService.getUser(id): map 404 to a not-found error and 5xx to a service-unavailable error, and handle a response that fails to decode.
expected_behavior: Catches HttpClientResponseException and branches on e.code() for 404 and 5xx
Catches HttpClientDecoderException for undecodable bodies
Notes that non-2xx responses throw HttpClientResponseException by default
Optionally uses @ResponseCodeMapper or HttpResponseEntity to avoid exceptions
Does not invent per-status exception classes such as NotFoundException or HttpStatusCodeException

evals.query: Add an API key to every request of a Kora HTTP client by reading the key from configuration and setting an X-API-Key header.
expected_behavior: Implements HttpClientInterceptor and overrides processRequest(Context, InterceptChain, HttpClientRequest)
Builds a new request with request.toBuilder().header(...).build() and calls chain.process
Registers the interceptor as a @Component and attaches it with @InterceptWith on the client
Reads the key from a @ConfigSource config interface, not a non-existent @ConfigValue
Uses constructor injection and does not use field injection or annotations from other frameworks

evals.query: Build a non-blocking Kora HTTP client whose methods return CompletionStage and fan out several calls in parallel.
expected_behavior: Declares @HttpRoute methods returning CompletionStage<T> with @Json where bodies are JSON
Composes results with thenApply/thenCompose and toCompletableFuture without blocking
Uses CompletableFuture.allOf to run calls in parallel and join the results
Mentions that Mono<T> (reactor-core) or Kotlin suspend are also supported signatures
Does not block inside the async chain with Thread.sleep or join in a callback

evals.query: Make a production Kora HTTP client resilient: retry GETs, a circuit breaker on POSTs, a timeout, and a fallback that returns an empty list.
expected_behavior: Adds the ru.tinkoff.kora:resilient-kora dependency and ResilientModule to @KoraApp
Annotates methods with @Retry("name"), @CircuitBreaker("name"), @Timeout("name")
Uses @Fallback(value = "name", method = "...") referencing a default fallback method
Puts the tuning values under the resilient.* config (retry/circuitbreaker/timeout blocks)
Does not use inline attributes like maxAttempts, failureThreshold, or a @Backoff annotation

evals.query: Send a plain-text request body instead of JSON from a Kora HTTP client, and decode different response shapes for 200 versus error status codes.
expected_behavior: Implements HttpClientRequestMapper<T> returning HttpBody.plaintext(...) and references it with @Mapping
Uses @ResponseCodeMapper(code = 200, ...) and @ResponseCodeMapper(code = ResponseCodeMapper.DEFAULT, ...)
Implements HttpClientResponseMapper<T> reading response.body().asInputStream() and response.code()
Models the result as a sealed interface with @Json record variants
Imports @Mapping from ru.tinkoff.kora.common.Mapping rather than inventing an annotation
